notes: For um- verbs, the infinitive and completed forms are identical.

examples.english: I want to eat rice porridge.
Go ahead and eat!
I already ate breakfast.
She ate fish yesterday.
We are eating lunch right now.
He eats fruit every day.
We will eat out tomorrow.
Will you eat sinigang?
Eat the vegetables.
He doesn't want to eat the bitter melon.
The child ate the candy.

examples.tagalog: Kinain ko ang ulam na inihanda ng nanay.
examples.english: I ate the dish that mother prepared.
They are eating dinner.
The dog is eating the leftovers.
We will eat the bibingka later.

examples.tagalog: Kakainin mo ba ang lahat ng iyon?
examples.english: Will you eat all of that?

examples.tagalog: Gusto naming kainan ang bagong restawran.
examples.english: We want to eat at the new restaurant.
Let's eat in the living room.

examples.tagalog: Kinainan namin ang restawran sa tabi ng simbahan.
examples.english: We ate at the restaurant beside the church.

examples.tagalog: Kinainan ng pamilya ang kusina ngayong umaga.
examples.english: The family ate in the kitchen this morning.

examples.tagalog: Kinakainan ng mga bisita ang terasa.
examples.english: The guests are eating on the terrace.

examples.tagalog: Kinakainan ng pamilya ang sala tuwing Linggo.
examples.english: The family eats in the living room every Sunday.

examples.tagalog: Kakainan namin ang bagong restawran bukas.
examples.english: We will eat at the new restaurant tomorrow.

examples.tagalog: Kakainan ng mga bata ang parke mamaya.
examples.english: The children will eat at the park later.
